description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a metal support structure. The solar panels are dark, likely monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon, and are arranged in a single row elevated above a base platform. The supporting frame appears to be made of metal, possibly aluminum or steel, with angled struts providing stability. The base platform beneath the panels is flat and patterned, possibly simulating a rooftop or ground surface, and includes geometric shapes and color variations. No visible electrical inverter or cabling is present, suggesting these components may be concealed or not modeled. The overall design is compact and suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility installations.
description: A compact solar panel array mounted on a metal frame above a patterned base platform, designed for urban solar energy collection.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the supporting angled metal frame above the patterned base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the installation, the tilt of the solar panels, and the structural supports beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels, the supporting frame, and the patterned base platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the panel tilt and the geometric pattern on the base, with the frame structure visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled aluminum or steel frame
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of 6 solar panels
